<!-- Generated by tutor-handouts. CUDA exercise seed bank (15 entries). -->

# CUDA Exercise Bank

15 exercise seeds covering the 6 CUDA topics from `cuda-tutor-setup`. Each
entry MUST have all 10 required fields (validated by
`scripts/validate-exercise-bank.py`).

Topic slugs: `cuda-kernels`, `cutlass`, `cutile`, `open-gpu-kernel-modules`,
`nccl`, `nvshmem`.

Difficulty notation: ★☆☆ Easy, ★★☆ Medium, ★★★ Hard.

---

### E-CUDA-01 Vector Add
- Topic: cuda-kernels
- Difficulty: ★☆☆
- Concepts exercised: grid/block decomposition, global memory access, bounds masking, host/device memory transfer
- Prerequisites: none
- Reference: CUDA C++ Programming Guide §2.2, OLCF cuda-training-series `exercises/hw1/vector_add.cu`
- Estimated learner time: 1–2 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 90% of `torch.add` throughput on N=2^24, FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 1, 1024, 65536, 4194304
- atol/rtol: 1e-5 / 1e-5
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute global tid from blockIdx/threadIdx; 2. bounds check tid < N; 3. perform the add

### E-CUDA-02 Tiled Matmul (Shared Memory)
- Topic: cuda-kernels
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: shared memory tiling, `__syncthreads`, thread coarsening, bank-conflict-free padding
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-01
- Reference: PMPP §4, CUDA C++ Programming Guide §3.2.4, CUTLASS `examples/00_basic_gemm/basic_gemm.cu`
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 60% of cuBLAS GEMM on 4096×4096 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 16, 256, 1024, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. load A tile into __shared__; 2. load B tile into __shared__; 3. __syncthreads barrier; 4. inner-product accumulate; 5. handle non-multiple-of-tile boundaries

### E-CUDA-03 Block Reduction
- Topic: cuda-kernels
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: warp shuffle, sequential addressing, sequential vs interleaved patterns, atomicAdd alternative
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-01
- Reference: Mark Harris "Optimizing Parallel Reduction in CUDA" (NVIDIA whitepaper 2007), CUDA samples `Samples/2_Concepts_and_Techniques/reduction/`
- Estimated learner time: 3–5 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 80% of `torch.sum` on N=2^22 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 1024, 65536, 1048576, 4194304
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. load thread-local partial sum into shared; 2. tree-reduction with sequential addressing; 3. warp-level shuffle for last 32 elements; 4. block-result atomicAdd to global

### E-CUDA-04 Prefix Sum (Scan)
- Topic: cuda-kernels
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: work-efficient scan (Blelloch), up-sweep/down-sweep, multi-block scan with auxiliary array
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-03
- Reference: Blelloch 1990, NVIDIA GPU Gems 3 Ch.39, CUB `device_scan.cuh`
- Estimated learner time: 8–12 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 70% of `torch.cumsum` on N=2^22 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 1024, 65536, 1048576, 4194304
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. up-sweep tree; 2. set root to identity; 3. down-sweep tree; 4. block-aggregate auxiliary scan; 5. apply auxiliary back

### E-CUDA-05 cp.async Pipelined Copy
- Topic: cuda-kernels
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: `cp.async.ca`, `cp.async.commit_group`, `cp.async.wait_group`, two-stage software pipeline
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-02
- Reference: CUDA C++ Programming Guide §B.27, PTX ISA 8.5 §9.7.13.5.10, CUTLASS `include/cute/atom/copy_atom.hpp`
- Estimated learner time: 8–12 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.4× speedup over E-CUDA-02 on 4096×4096 GEMM on A100 (sm_80)
- Test sizes: 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. issue cp.async for tile N+1 while computing tile N; 2. commit_group after each batch; 3. wait_group(1) to drain depth-1; 4. double-buffer __shared__ tile array

### E-CUTLASS-01 CuTe Layout and Stride
- Topic: cutlass
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `cute::Layout`, `cute::Stride`, `cute::Shape`, hierarchical tiling, `print_layout`
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-02
- Reference: CUTLASS `media/docs/cute/00_quickstart.md`, `include/cute/layout.hpp`
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: N/A — correctness exercise; verify with `print_layout` output match
- Test sizes: 4×4, 16×16, 64×64, 256×256
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. construct row-major Layout for an MxN tile; 2. compose with a column-major partition; 3. apply a Swizzle<3,3,3>; 4. verify with `print_layout`

### E-CUTLASS-02 CUTLASS GEMM with MMA Atom
- Topic: cutlass
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: `cutlass::gemm::device::Gemm`, MMA atom selection, `Stages`, `ThreadblockSwizzle`, alignment
- Prerequisites: E-CUTLASS-01
- Reference: CUTLASS `examples/00_basic_gemm/basic_gemm.cu`, `media/docs/efficient_gemm.md`
- Estimated learner time: 6–10 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 95% of cuBLAS GEMM on 4096×4096 FP16 on H100 (sm_90)
- Test sizes: 256, 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. select MMA atom for sm_90 FP16; 2. set Stages=4 for cp.async pipelining; 3. configure ThreadblockSwizzle for L2 reuse; 4. verify alignment of A, B, C

### E-CUTLASS-03 WGMMA Hopper Kernel
- Topic: cutlass
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: WGMMA (warp-group matrix multiply), `wgmma.mma_async` PTX, TMA descriptor setup, warp-specialization
- Prerequisites: E-CUTLASS-02
- Reference: CUTLASS `examples/48_hopper_warp_specialized_gemm/`, PTX ISA 8.5 §9.7.13.5.18 (WGMMA)
- Estimated learner time: 12–16 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.5× speedup over E-CUTLASS-02 (sm_80 MMA) on 4096×4096 FP16 on H100 (sm_90)
- Test sizes: 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. set up TMA tensor descriptors for A, B; 2. launch warp-specialized kernel with producer/consumer warpgroups; 3. issue wgmma.mma_async; 4. wgmma.commit_group + wait_group; 5. epilogue store

### E-CUTILE-01 cuTile Matmul
- Topic: cutile
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: cuTile DSL, tile shape declaration, `@tile.kernel`, host wrapper, autotuning
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-02
- Reference: NVIDIA cuTile documentation, `cutile-python` examples
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 70% of cuBLAS GEMM on 4096×4096 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 256, 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. declare tile shape (M_TILE, N_TILE, K_TILE); 2. write the `@tile.kernel`; 3. wrap in host launcher; 4. autotune over tile shapes

### E-OGKM-01 RM Init Trace
- Topic: open-gpu-kernel-modules
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: Resource Manager initialization, `RmInitAdapter`, `nv_state_t`, ftrace-based call tracing
- Prerequisites: none (read-only analysis exercise)
- Reference: open-gpu-kernel-modules `src/nvidia/src/kernel/rmapi/`, `kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c`
- Estimated learner time: 6–8 hours
- Performance target: N/A — produce a labeled call-graph diagram
- Test sizes: N/A (analysis exercise; deliverable is a diagram + 1-page writeup)
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. identify modprobe entry point; 2. trace through to RmInitAdapter; 3. document GSP-load decision branch; 4. produce labeled diagram

### E-OGKM-02 GSP RPC Inspection
- Topic: open-gpu-kernel-modules
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: GSP firmware boot, RPC channel setup, `kgspBootstrap_IMPL`, NVGSP-RM split
- Prerequisites: E-OGKM-01
- Reference: open-gpu-kernel-modules `src/nvidia/src/kernel/gpu/gsp/`, NVIDIA GSP whitepaper
- Estimated learner time: 8–12 hours
- Performance target: N/A — produce a labeled RPC trace
- Test sizes: N/A (analysis exercise; deliverable is a sequence diagram)
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. locate kgspBootstrap entry; 2. trace RPC queue setup; 3. identify command/message buffer layout; 4. produce sequence diagram

### E-NCCL-01 Custom Ring AllReduce
- Topic: nccl
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: ring topology, send/recv primitives, reduction chunking, `ncclAllReduce` semantics
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-01, E-CUDA-03
- Reference: NCCL paper (Sanders 2009 for ring), NCCL source `src/collectives/ring.cc`
- Estimated learner time: 6–8 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 60% of `ncclAllReduce` bandwidth on 4×A100 NVLink topology
- Test sizes: 1MB, 16MB, 256MB, 1GB
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute ring rank ordering; 2. issue chunked send/recv; 3. perform reduce-scatter phase; 4. perform allgather phase

### E-NCCL-02 NVLink-SHARP Throughput Measurement
- Topic: nccl
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: NVLink-SHARP (NVLS), tree topology, switch reduction offload, `NCCL_ALGO=NVLS`
- Prerequisites: E-NCCL-01
- Reference: NCCL user guide §"Algorithms", H100 NVLink-Switch System whitepaper
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.3× speedup over ring on 8×H100 NVL with 256MB AllReduce
- Test sizes: 16MB, 256MB, 1GB, 4GB
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. enable NCCL_ALGO=NVLS in env; 2. measure ring baseline; 3. measure NVLS run; 4. report bandwidth table

### E-NVSHMEM-01 Symmetric Heap put/get
- Topic: nvshmem
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `nvshmem_malloc`, `nvshmem_put`, `nvshmem_get`, `nvshmem_barrier_all`, symmetric heap addressing
- Prerequisites: E-CUDA-01
- Reference: NVSHMEM API guide §3 (memory model), NVSHMEM examples `examples/put_block.cu`
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 50 GB/s on intra-node 8×A100 NVLink (per-PE put)
- Test sizes: 1KB, 64KB, 4MB, 256MB
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. allocate symmetric buffer on each PE; 2. issue put from PE 0 to PE 1; 3. barrier_all; 4. verify on PE 1

### E-NVSHMEM-02 IBGDA On-Stream Collective
- Topic: nvshmem
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: IBGDA (InfiniBand GPUDirect Async), `nvshmemx_on_stream_*` APIs, GPU-initiated RDMA
- Prerequisites: E-NVSHMEM-01
- Reference: NVSHMEM API guide §"On-Stream API", IBGDA whitepaper (NVIDIA 2022)
- Estimated learner time: 6–10 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 80% of CPU-initiated AllReduce on 2-node 16×H100 IB
- Test sizes: 1MB, 16MB, 256MB
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. enable IBGDA at init; 2. issue nvshmemx_putmem_on_stream; 3. issue nvshmemx_quiet_on_stream; 4. measure vs CPU-initiated baseline
